meta.summary: All 10 ships verified against screenshots. All core stats match existing JSON. Minor additions noted for descriptions and some missing fields.

results.name: Balloon

screenshotExtracted.name: Balloon
screenshotExtracted.unlockRequirement: Faction Decree (rep 34/100), Horn of Plenty (10M gold deals), Armada (12 legends collected)
screenshotExtracted.description: A marvelous invention of 1783. You can control altitude, speed, and direction, but cannot move against the wind. The balloon can be attacked and risks crashing into mountain terrain.

missingInJson: description text not captured in JSON
results.notes: No weapon slots, hull dimensions, displacement, or integrity shown in screenshot — consistent with JSON having none of these fields. All existing stats match perfectly.

screenshotExtracted.description: The "turtle ship" served as the core of the Korean fleet during the 16th and 17th centuries. In addition to her unique covered design, she wields combined armament, including mortars.

results.name: Le Requin

screenshotExtracted.name: Le Requin
screenshotExtracted.description: French frigates and combat sloops were ineffective against the Berber pirates, who used the agile xebecs. Logically speaking, if they are able to make such good use of them, then why not try those xebecs ourselves? The original name, meaning "Shark" when translated from French, was launched in the year 1751 in Toulon, where it soon made the lives of those pirates more difficult.

missingInJson: description text not captured in JSON
results.notes: All stats match existing JSON perfectly. Ship is fully researched in screenshot.

results.name: Polacca

screenshotExtracted.name: Polacca
screenshotExtracted.description: The Venetian fleet kept armed polaccas in service to protect trade routes in the Adriatic Sea from Dalmatian, Greek, and Turkish pirates.

results.name: 12 Apostolov

screenshotExtracted.name: 12 Apostolov

cost.note: Costs hidden - previous ship not researched

screenshotExtracted.description: A ship of the line of the Black Sea Fleet, it participated in the defense of Sevastopol during the Crimean War. It was scuttled along with other ships on February 13, 1855, to block Anglo-French ships from entering the harbor. Fortunately, the Archipelago's shipwrights managed to recover its blueprints.

missingInJson: description text not captured in JSON

cost.note: Costs hidden - previous ship not researched

screenshotExtracted.description: A famed French ship of the line that duelled with HMS Victory during the Battle of Trafalgar. The fatal shot killing Vice-Admiral Horatio Nelson was fired from this very ship.

results.name: Constitution

screenshotExtracted.name: Constitution

cost.note: Costs hidden - previous ship not researched

screenshotExtracted.description: One of the original six frigates of the United States Navy, the "Constitution" was larger and more heavily armed than its contemporaries. She gained fame during the War of 1812 against Great Britain, earning the nickname "Old Ironsides" and the adoration of many.
